rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. QUAYLE TRUST DATED NOV. 11, 1980
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: An appearance is required. The Court is to determine the following issues: 1) Should the petitioner be allowed credit for tangible personal assets of which the beneficiaries denied having received? 2) How should Marky Quayle's right to be reimbursed from the trust property be satisfied? 3) Should the petitioner be allowed compensation for his trustee services at his attorney rate? *As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 285 352 2795 Password: 715034 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the ID and password above. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: EUNICE JULSON POOLED SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/17/2020. 1) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The verification is dated 03/13/2019, prior to the account ending date. B) Are all the ETFs purchased during the account period authorized investments under Probate Code § 2574(a)? If so, did the petitioner comply with LRSF 14.78(J)(7) requirng the Court's authorization for ETFs? C) Paragraph 12 of the petition alleges that the petitioner received $1,000 on 12/03/2018, but Schedule C shows the payment of $2,000 ($1,000 allocated to principal and $1,000 to income). D) Paragraph 15 of the petition does not identify any remainder/contingent beneficiaries. See CRC 7.902 & Probate Code § 15804. E) The allocation of cash and non-cash assets as of the account ending date in the summary of account does not match the allocation on Schedule E. F) Why did the petitioner allocate the two federal taxe payments 100% to principal? G) The total amount of compensation of the petitioner's attorney is incorrectly calculated on Exhibit F. Also, the year of the attorney's signing the declaration is missing. H) The current bond and sufficiency thereof must be addressed in all interim accounts. LRSF 14.63. I) A statement of compensation required by Probate Code § 1064(a)(3) must be provided. J) The address of the conservator's attorney listed in the proof of service appears to be the old address. The address change notice was filed on 07/19/2019 in the conservatorship case. K) Is the conservator's address listed in the proof of service accurate? 2) Notice must be given to remainder/contingent beneficiaries. A supplemental proof of service must be filed. *The conservatorship case number must be noted in parentheses under any pleading caption in this related trust case. LRSF 14.78(A)(7)(c). **As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 285 352 2795 Password: 715034 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the ID and password above. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALLURA FONG SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #12, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) Petitioner Adora Fong purchased a vehicle from the trust. 2) Petitioners request $97,240 for their caregiver services during this two-year accounting period. 3) Court to determine whether attorneys fees should be granted as requested, which includes the following entries: a. Attorney Fees (Attorney Ross) i. Paralegal ($150/hr): conference w/ messenger, obtaining check for filing fee, preparing documents for filing, revising computer templates to update new case matter. ii. Paralegal: download e-filed copy of 11th account, make courtesy copies and deliver to court. iii. Paralegal: total of $180 for checking tentative ruling 6 times on one hearing. iv. Paralegal: ~$6,000 charged for addressing Examiner's Notes, which appear to be mostly due to errors and omissions. v. Attorney ($520/hr): prepared for and attended hearing: 4 hours = $2,080. b. Attorney Fees (Attorney Robb) i. Paralegal ($150/hr): ~$750 charged to increase bond. As a result of the covid-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th amended general order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 285 352 2795 Password: 715034 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Co-Trustee Anthony Dintcho To Account, To Confirm That Co-Trustee Anthony Dintcho Is No Longer A Co-Trustee; And For Surcharge
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Remote appearance via Court Call or Zoom encouraged. No new hearing notes will be sent as nothing new has been filed since the last hearing. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CATHERINE WHITCOMB TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And For Imposition Of Constructive Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/10/2020. The following issues must be resolved: 1) No courtesy copy of the subject petition has been received by the assigned examiner. 2) No proof of service of notice of the hearing is on file. 3) An ex parte petition for an order consolidating this matter with the prior proceeding involving the same trust and issues (PTR-19-302527) must be filed under the lower number. *As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 285 352 2795 Password: 715034 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the ID and password above. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY NGAI YING CHEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/5/20 (no earlier availability). The will names both Ming Yu Wu and Yan Miao Wu as alternate co-executor; Yan Miao Wu's declination to serve has not been filed. Item 8 of the petition does not indicate who the trustee or beneficiaries of the devisee trust are and, if the trustee is the petitioner, all trust beneficiaries must be clearly identified and must receive notice. Item 3f(2) of the petition is incomplete. Item 3g(1)(a) should be checked, not 3g(1)(c) because the petitioner is named as an alternate executor.
